page: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256gb is an x86_64 server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical AMD EPYC cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes 1 GB of network-attached local storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address, with no integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory performance, with a PassMark memory score in the top 10% and large-block memory write bandwidth reaching 196.72 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression both rank in the top 10%, achieving 40.57 GB/sec and 18.14 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng falls within the middle 50%, while PassMark CPU scores, database operations, and Redis SET operations (63.76 million operations per second) consistently rank in the top 25%.

The server offers high memory density relative to its shared CPU allocation, presenting a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. However, the shared CPU model and the minimal 1 GB of network-attached local storage represent key tradeoffs, requiring external storage solutions for data-heavy applications. This configuration is suitable for large-scale caching, in-memory databases, static web serving, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256gb is an x86_64 server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 32 shared vCPUs on 16 physical AMD EPYC cores running at 2.0 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, alongside 1 GB of network-attached storage and no GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier memory write bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong database and Redis performance. However, the shared CPU allocation and minimal local storage are notable tradeoffs. This server is suited for memory-intensive workloads, in-memory databases, and static web serving.

tagline: A high-density memory instance featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and exceptional memory bandwidth for memory-bound workloads.
